payload.title: Event-sourced storage: git-mergeable, deploy-free collaboration
payload.description:
Make the append-only operation log the source of truth and treat scope.db as a rebuildable local cache, so divergent replicas merge by *union of events* instead of an impossible 3-way merge of binary SQLite pages.

## Why
Today the materialized snapshot (tickets/relations/comments rows) is the source of truth, and that is what any file-sync transport tries to merge. A snapshot is the current answer with the path discarded, so two divergent answers have no common ancestor to reconcile against — committing scope.db to git corrupts it on merge.

We already keep `ticket_history` (field, old_value, new_value, changed_by, changed_at): an event log in all but name. If we promote an append-only op stream to source-of-truth and rebuild the db by replaying it, then:
- Appending is union-mergeable: merge = union of both peers' events, replayed in (changed_at, changed_by) order. Nothing to corrupt.
- Transport becomes any dumb file sync — git pull, iCloud, Dropbox, Syncthing — with ZERO server to deploy.
- Decentralized / local-first is preserved (every peer is a full offline replica; eventual consistency is the honest definition of decentralized).
- LAN real-time is retained via the existing mTLS hub/pairing; peers can gossip events directly, still no central host.

## Out of scope
Sub-second real-time across the open internet with zero infrastructure — that mathematically requires a meeting point and is not a goal here.

## Acceptance
A second clone can `git pull` a divergent set of ticket changes and end up with an identical, uncorrupted board after replay, with no SQLite file ever shared.
